segments.text: First listen, and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What do glimpses of beauty, either in nature or art, suggest to the human mind?

translations.zh-Hans: 无论自然还是艺术中的美之片刻，会使人想到什么？

segments.text: A young man sees a sunset, and, unable to understand or to express the emotion that it rouses in him, concludes that it must be the gateway to a world that lies beyond.

translations.zh-Hans: 一个年轻人看见日落，无法理解或表达它在心中激起的情感，便断定它一定是通往彼岸世界的大门。

segments.text: It is difficult for any of us, in moments of intense aesthetic experience, to resist the suggestion that we are catching a glimpse of a light that shines down to us from a different realm of existence.

translations.zh-Hans: 当我们经历强烈的审美体验时，很难抗拒这样的暗示：仿佛瞥见一道从另一存在领域照向我们的光。

segments.text: Different, and, because the experience is intensely moving, in some way higher.

translations.zh-Hans: 那个领域不同于这里；又因为这种体验极其动人，它在某种意义上也更高。

segments.text: And though the gleams blind and dazzle, yet do they convey a hint of beauty and serenity greater than we have known or imagined, greater, too, than we can describe, for language which was invented to convey the meanings of

translations.zh-Hans: 这些光芒虽令人目眩，却暗示着一种比我们所知或所想更伟大的美与宁静，也比我们所能描述的更伟大，因为语言本为传达

segments.text: this world cannot readily be fitted to the uses of another.

translations.zh-Hans: 这个世界的意义而发明，很难直接用于另一个世界。

segments.text: That all great art has this power of suggesting a world beyond is undeniable.

translations.zh-Hans: 一切伟大艺术都有暗示彼岸世界的力量，这是不可否认的。

segments.text: In some moods, nature shares it.

translations.zh-Hans: 在某些心境中，大自然也具有这种力量。

segments.text: There is no sky and june so blue that it does not point forward to a bluer.

translations.zh-Hans: 六月的天空无论多蓝，都会让人想到还有更蓝的天空。

segments.text: No sunset so beautiful that it does not waken the vision of a greater beauty, a vision which passes before it is fully glimpsed, and in passing leaves an indefinable longing and regret.

translations.zh-Hans: 日落无论多美，都会唤起对更伟大之美的幻象；还没完全看清，幻象便已消逝，离去时留下一种难以言明的渴望和遗憾。

segments.text: But if this world is not merely a bad joke, life a vulgar flare amid the cool radiance of the stars, and existence an empty laugh braying across the mysteries, if these intimations of a something behind and

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，如果这个世界不只是一场恶劣的玩笑，生命不只是繁星清冷光辉间一团庸俗的火焰，存在也不只是响彻神秘宇宙的一阵空洞狂笑；如果这些关于事物背后和

segments.text: beyond are not evil humor born of indigestion, or whimsies sent by the devil to mock and madness, if, in a word, beauty means something, yet we must not seek to interpret the meaning.

translations.zh-Hans: 彼岸尚有某种存在的暗示，不是消化不良产生的恶意幽默，也不是魔鬼派来嘲弄人、使人发疯的怪念；总之，如果美确有意义，我们仍不应试图解释这种意义。

segments.text: If we glimpse the unutterable, it is unwise to try to utter it, nor should we seek to invest with significance that which we cannot grasp.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果我们瞥见了不可言说之物，企图把它说出来是不明智的；我们也不应试图赋予自己无法把握之物某种意义。

segments.text: Beauty, in terms of our human meanings, is meaningless.

translations.zh-Hans: 以人类的意义衡量，美是没有意义的。
